scenario: A team's Job Worker for send-email was scheduled to run with at-least-once delivery. Zeebe re-activates a job after timeout; the worker may receive the same job twice. The team wants to ensure emails aren't sent twice to the customer.
question: Which best practice fits?

options.text: Increase the job timeout duration so the worker always completes before the timeout expires and Zeebe never re-activates the job. Increasing timeout to avoid retries hides the underlying delivery guarantee issue and creates slow incident detection when a worker genuinely fails. Very long timeouts mean stuck jobs stay invisible in monitoring for extended periods. At-least-once delivery is inherent in Zeebe's design regardless of timeout configuration.

, the job's elementInstanceKey) when calling the email API. The email service deduplicates requests with the same key within a time window, ensuring that even if the worker receives the job twice, only one email is sent to the customer. Idempotency is the canonical pattern for at-least-once delivery systems.

Configure Zeebe for exactly-once delivery semantics by setting an engine configuration flag that prevents duplicate job activations. Zeebe provides at-least-once delivery as an architectural invariant; there is no configuration option to change this to exactly-once delivery. The distributed nature of Zeebe's job activation mechanism means that under timeout or network scenarios, a job may be activated more than once, requiring workers to implement idempotency.

Configure the worker to not retry on failure -- set job retries to zero so Zeebe never attempts re-activation after an initial failure. Disabling retries eliminates the duplicate delivery risk but destroys the reliability guarantees that make at-least-once delivery valuable. Any transient failure -- network blip, service restart, or temporary API unavailability -- would immediately create an incident, degrading the system's fault tolerance and operational resilience.

a.text: Incorrect. Zeebe's at-least-once semantics mean workers must be idempotent. Use a unique key per job (e.g., elementInstanceKey or a business-level id) when calling external systems; the system deduplicates.

b.text: Correct. Long timeouts hide the issue and create slow incident detection.

c.text: Incorrect. Zeebe is at-least-once by design; no "exactly-once" config flag.

d.text: Incorrect. Disabling retries undermines reliability guarantees.

explanation: Long timeouts hide the issue and create slow incident detection.
